output.title: Landlord Withholding Security Deposit? Demand It Back in 60 Seconds
output.meta_description: Landlord withholding your security deposit? Demand it back with a legally sound letter in 60 seconds. Only $19.99 at Deposits-Back.com—no signup needed.
content_outline: Why Your Landlord Is Withholding Your Security Deposit (And Why It's Often Illegal)
What Your Landlord Can and Cannot Deduct: Tenant Rights Explained
The First Step Most Tenants Skip: Sending a Formal Demand Letter
How to Generate a Deposit Demand Letter in 60 Seconds Without a Lawyer
What to Do If Your Landlord Still Refuses to Return Your Deposit
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14–30 days after move-out.
Normal wear and tear, minor scuffs, and standard cleaning cannot be legally deducted from your deposit.
A formal demand letter creates a paper trail and often triggers faster repayment before small claims court is necessary.
Deposits-Back.com lets U.S. renters generate and send a legally sound deposit return demand letter in 60 seconds for $19.99 with no signup or subscription required.
output.cta: Stop chasing your landlord. Get your money back today. Visit Deposits-Back.com and send your deposit demand letter in 60 seconds for just $19.99—no signup, no subscription, no stress.
output.target_audience: Problem-aware U.S. renters who are frustrated because their landlord is withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit and need a fast, affordable solution.
tiktok_hooks: POV: You moved out 3 weeks ago and your landlord is suddenly 'too busy' to return your deposit 😒
Your landlord cannot keep your security deposit for 'normal wear and tear'—and most don't want you to know that.
If your landlord hasn't returned your deposit in 30 days, they might actually owe you DOUBLE.
My landlord tried to charge me $800 for 'dust.' I sent one letter and got my full deposit back.
Stop texting your landlord. This 60-second letter is what actually works.
